page: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, providing a total of 640 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor offering 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features 1360.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 14.17 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25%. However, multi-core capabilities are highly robust, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Memory read bandwidth is also highly efficient, scoring in the top 25% for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks, while write bandwidth and database operations perform in the average range. Redis SET operations demonstrate strong throughput, reaching the top 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents an extreme resource-density profile designed for massive parallel workloads. The lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning, which is a key architectural tradeoff. While single-threaded tasks are inefficient on this hardware class due to the lower 2.2 GHz base clock speed, the massive GPU and memory allocation delivers high qualitative value for workloads that can fully exploit parallel execution.

This instance is engineered specifically for workloads requiring massive parallel acceleration and substantial memory footprints. Suitable applications include large-scale deep learning training, high-performance computing simulations, complex scientific modeling, and memory-intensive parallel data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for massive parallel computing workloads. It features 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a total of 640 GB of VRAM, paired with an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor providing 96 vCPUs and 1360.0 GB of system memory. The architecture lacks local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Benchmarks indicate weak single-core performance but strong multi-core and memory read bandwidth capabilities, placing it in the top 25% for parallel processing tasks. This server is highly optimized for deep learning, parallel scientific simulations, and large-scale data processing workloads that can leverage its extensive GPU array and substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 16 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 1360 GB of RAM, engineered for massive parallel computing and deep learning.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g features 16 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 1360 GB RAM for parallel computing and deep learning.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring sixteen GPUs and massive system memory for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads.

96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1360 GB of system memory
